headlines.title: Yen nears four-decade low, and Katayama issues warning
Nikkei Index Soars Over 70,000 for First Time
World shares are mixed and US futures fall after a tech-led rally on Wall St
Bank of Japan chief Ueda discharged from hospital after missing policy meeting
Bank of Japan chief Ueda discharged from hospital
Japanese Yen: Uptrend intact as 162 resistance nears – Societe Generale
Japan's Nikkei posts sharpest weekly gain in nearly two years on AI shares rally

marketTone.ko: 일본 증시는 인공지능(AI) 관련주 강세에 힘입어 닛케이 지수가 사상 처음으로 70,000선을 돌파하며 약 2년 만에 가장 큰 주간 상승률을 기록했습니다. 반면 엔화는 40년 만의 최저치에 근접하며 가타야마 의원의 경고를 받았고, 전문가들은 엔화 약세 추세가 162엔 저항선에 다가서고 있다고 분석했습니다. 우에다 일본은행 총재가 퇴원했음에도 불구하고, 글로벌 증시가 혼조세를 보이고 미국 선물 지수가 하락하는 등 시장 전반의 복합적인 상황이 이어지고 있습니다.
marketTone.en: The Japanese stock market saw the Nikkei Index soar past 70,000 for the first time, marking its sharpest weekly gain in nearly two years, largely driven by a rally in AI shares. This strong performance comes amidst a significantly weak yen, which is nearing a four-decade low and has prompted warnings from officials like Katayama, with analysts noting its uptrend remains intact as 162 resistance approaches. While Bank of Japan chief Ueda has been discharged from the hospital, global shares are mixed and US futures are falling after a tech-led rally, presenting a nuanced backdrop for the market.
